threats.name: Linux Kernel Incorrect Resource Transfer Between Spheres Vulnerability
threats.description: Linux Kernel contains an incorrect resource transfer between spheres vulnerability that could allow for privilege escalation.

threats.name: WebPros cPanel & WHM and WP2 (WordPress Squared) Missing Authentication for Critical Function Vulnerability
threats.description: WebPros cPanel & WHM (WebHost Manager) and WP2 (WordPress Squared) contain an authentication bypass vulnerability in the login flow that allows unauthenticated remote attackers to gain unauthorized access to the control panel.

threats.name: ConnectWise ScreenConnect Path Traversal Vulnerability
threats.description: ConnectWise ScreenConnect contains a path traversal vulnerability which could allow an attacker to execute remote code or directly impact confidential data and critical systems.

threats.name: Microsoft Windows Protection Mechanism Failure Vulnerability
threats.description: Microsoft Windows Shell contains a protection mechanism failure vulnerability that allows an unauthorized attacker to perform spoofing over a network.

threats.name: D-Link DIR-823X Command Injection Vulnerability
threats.description: D-Link DIR-823X contains a command injection vulnerability that allows an authorized attacker to execute arbitrary commands on remote devices by sending a POST request to /goform/set_prohibiting via the corresponding function. The impacted product could be end-of-life (EoL) and/or end-of-service (EoS). Users should discontinue product utilization.

threats.name: Samsung MagicINFO 9 Server Path Traversal Vulnerability
threats.description: Samsung MagicINFO 9 Server contains a path traversal vulnerability that could allow an attacker to write arbitrary files as system authority.

threats.name: SimpleHelp Path Traversal Vulnerability
threats.description: SimpleHelp contains a path traversal vulnerability that allows admin users to upload arbitrary files anywhere on the file system by uploading a crafted zip file (i.e. zip slip). This can be exploited to execute arbitrary code on the host in the context of the SimpleHelp server user.

threats.name: SimpleHelp Missing Authorization Vulnerability
threats.description: SimpleHelp contains a missing authorization vulnerability that could allow low-privileged technicians to create API keys with excessive permissions. These API keys can be used to escalate privileges to the server admin role.

threats.name: Next.js may leak x-middleware-subrequest-id to external hosts
threats.description: Next.js may leak x-middleware-subrequest-id to external hosts

threats.name: Information exposure in Next.js dev server due to lack of origin verification
threats.description: Information exposure in Next.js dev server due to lack of origin verification

threats.name: Next.js: Unbounded next/image disk cache growth can exhaust storage
threats.description: Next.js: Unbounded next/image disk cache growth can exhaust storage
